title: #eng-releases: 6790012345
preview:
Alice Nguyen: Kicking off private on-prem rollout v3.4.0 to cluster oak-2 in the maintenance window now. FYI deploybot autosuppress is ON for scheduled backups.
DeployBot: [system] Canary slice started for v3.4.0 on oak-2. Metrics: p95=180ms -> 520ms, error-rate=0.8% (threshold 0.5%). Auto-rollback suppressed due to maintenance_flag=true (backup job overlap).
Ben Carter: Seeing 5xx spike from oak-2-app-1 in logs, correlation with backup window looks strong.
Carol Singh: If backup is causing IO contention we might be getting a false positive. Should we lift autosuppress and let the system rollback, or do a manual pin/rollback?
Dana Lopez: Oncall for oak-2 here — checking node metrics. Disk IO for node-3 at 95%, backup id bk-2026-03-22-1 running since 6790012000.
Eve Martinez: Recommend manual isolation: shift traffic off oak-2 app nodes and pin private deployment to v3.3.9 until morning. Safer than lifting autosuppress mid-backup.
Ben Carter: Pin command (if y'all agree): `redwood pin-version --deployment private --version v3.3.9 --scope oak-2`
Alice Nguyen: Running pin now and opening ops comms.
Alice Nguyen: ```
> redwood pin-version --deployment private --version v3.3.9 --scope oak-2
Output: OK — pinned v3.3.9 for scope oak-2, traffic shifted 65% -> 100% to pinned variant, rolling restarts scheduled.
```
DeployBot: Manual pin action recorded by alice in audit log (action_id: act-6790012350). Auto-rollback remains suppressed (maintenance flag).
Dana Lopez: Traffic shift completed. Current p95=165ms, error-rate=0.2%. Node IO dropping as backup finishes.
Carol Singh: Drafting a short release-note blurb for #release-notes: "Temporary rollback of private on-prem to v3.3.9 in EU (oak-2) during maintenance window due to maintenance-induced performance instability. No customer data impact. Investigating." Will post in 5.
Ben Carter: Filing incident for tracking: IR-20260322-1 with logs and backup window overlap callouts.
Alice Nguyen: Thanks team. Action items: (1) leave pinned until post-maintenance validation tomorrow morning, (2) eng-sre to investigate backup scheduling vs maintenance windows, (3) product comm to publish the blurb once Carol posts.
Eve Martinez: Follow-up suggestion: update DeployBot to include suppressed-reason metadata (backup job id + duration) in alerts so ops can correlate faster :+1:
oncall-bot: Incident IR-20260322-1 created and assigned to eng-sre (https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/IR-20260322-1).

title: #eng-releases: 6161616161
preview:
Alice Wang: heads up — noticed higher 95th latency on canary traffic after router patch. runtime still on v2.3.1, router bumped to v2.4.0 earlier today.
Ben Carter: ugh, which patch? the header-split one? :thinking_face: 
Alice Wang: yeah header-split + conn-pool tweak. console and api are on v2.3.x pinned by the rollout. router autoshift landed in prod.
Carol Nguyen: deploy-bot reported successful wave for router at 6161616100.
deploy-bot: wave completed: router v2.4.0 -> 100% (no errors). logs: /deploys/6161616100
Dan R.: how big is the 95th bump? numbers?
Alice Wang: from ~120ms -> ~210ms for tokenization-heavy requests. p50 unaffected.
Erin S.: could be header parsing change moving some work into runtime path due to header normalization. runtime now handles extra validation.
Ben Carter: we planned compatibility but forgot the runtime schema bump — oops. Is there a quick mitigation? pin router back, or roll runtime forward?
Alice Wang: runtime upgrade will take longer (model-serving infra). pinning router back is fastest.
Carol Nguyen: release notes for router include breaking change in header format. should've added a soft flag. I'll draft a note.
Dan R.: suggested rollback window? change window still open for another 45m.
Alice Wang: rollback router to v2.3.5 in next 10m unless anyone objects.
Ben Carter: +1
Erin S.: +1, also add a temporary routing rule to send canary % to runtime v2.3.1 only.
Carol Nguyen: I'll kick off pin and add a console banner and slack notify.
deploy-bot: starting rollback: router v2.4.0 -> v2.3.5 (initiated by carol nguyen)
Alice Wang: monitoring... p95 dropping back toward baseline.
Ben Carter: good catch. action items: 1) runtime team to prioritize schema fix; 2) router team to make header change behind feature flag; 3) we need clearer release notes checklist.
Carol Nguyen: I'll update release notes template to include 'compatibility matrix' and 'rollback runbook' checklist.
Erin S.: will open runtime PR for schema fix, target hotfix branch.
Dan R.: once runtime hotfix is validated we can re-roll router behind flag in next quiet window.
Alice Wang: thanks all. I'll annotate the deploy notes and create follow-up runbook in #eng-releases.
Carol Nguyen: posted banner and updated rollout notes. closing thread once metrics stable :thumbsup:

title: #eng-releases: 5943210001
preview:
deploybot: ALERT: prod-canary v4.0.0 exceeded rollback thresholds (p95 x2.4, errors 5.9%). Suggest automated rollback to v3.9.7 in 5m unless overridden. details: https://dash.redwood/prod-canary/594321
Maya Chen: Holding automation for 5m. We have EU-dedicated pools pinned to v4.0.0 with active customers; prefer scoped rollback.
Ethan Park: Proposal: rollback hosted and new-session traffic to v3.9.7, keep existing dedicated sessions pinned. That keeps customer sessions stable.
Sofia Ruiz: sounds good. Ethan, can you run the rollback? I'll draft release-note text and ping CS.
Ethan Park: Running partial rollback now. Command:
```
deployctl rollout --env=prod --service=hosted-api --to=v3.9.7 --traffic-slice=hosted --grace=30s --reason="canary-p95+errors"
```
ops-bot: CREATED RW-13321 (rollback exec) owner=Ethan Park.
deploybot: rollout job rb-594321 started -> hosted slice -> v3.9.7. ETA ~90s.
Ethan Park: smoke tests OK (50/50). p95 trending down, errors to 1.1%. Holding 15m for validation.
Sofia Ruiz: release-notes draft: "2026-03-21: Partial rollback hosted API v4.0.0 -> v3.9.7 due to KV-cache memory retries; dedicated pools remain pinned to v4.0.0." I'll post after hold.
Maya Chen: Ping CS: @cs-team heads-up in #customer-success-drafts. Note: dedicated unaffected. Open RW-13322 to track guard fix.
ops-bot: ROLLBACK COMPLETE rb-594321 by Ethan Park. Monitoring window 15m.
deploybot: Telemetry update: p95 normalized, error rate back to baseline.
Maya Chen: Thumbs. Schedule quick postmortem after monitoring window to assign RCA and mitigation items (RW-13321, RW-13322).

title: #eng-releases: 4109876543
preview:
Maria Chen: Heads-up — RC cut is tomorrow 09:00 PST. We need coordinated version bumps across api, runtime, console, and SDKs. Please drop PR links + 1–2 changelog bullets here. Any blockers?

Ajay Patel: Quick Q — are we pinning runtime to v4.1.0 or doing v4.1.1-rc for this cut?

Sophia Ruiz: I'll take api + console. API PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/api/pull/1876 (bump v1.12.0)
Console PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/console/pull/542 (bump v2.4.0)
Changelog bullets: "fix: normalize auth header parsing", "chore: surface token-level latency in route table"

release-bot: Deploy window scheduled: 2026-03-19 09:00 PST → 09:30 PST. Branch freeze at 2026-03-19 08:30 PST. Please add label `ready-for-cut` when merged.

Ben Carter: Changelog PR ready: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/333 — wording suggestions? leaning toward "fix: auth header normalization" (keeps scope narrow).

Olivia Park: SDKs: PR for JS client: https://github.com/redwood-inference/sdk-js/pull/210 (bump v0.9.1) — note: default timeout changed (10000 -> 15000ms) which is a mild break for folks relying on older default. I'll add migration note in the changelog.

Nathan Li: Runtime: holding off on the new quant kernel landing until after cut. Runtime PR (compat fixes + bump): https://github.com/redwood-inference/runtime/pull/998 — ran local compat tests: 120/123 passed, failing on softmax precision in longseq mode. Investigating, but should be fixable before 06:30 PST.

Ajay Patel: Console e2e — can someone confirm staging runs? I don't want a regression in rollout UI.

Sophia Ruiz: Console e2e passed on staging at 02:33 PST (run #712). No regressions observed on the route UI or rollout modal.

Maria Chen: Action items: @Sophia — merge API + console PRs by 05:00 PST. @Olivia — merge SDKs by 06:00 PST (include migration note). @Nathan — rebase runtime, push a compat fix and rerun tests by 06:30 PST. @Ben — finalize changelog wording and link to merged PRs. Ping me when each is merged. :thumbsup:

release-bot: Label `ready-for-cut` added to: https://github.com/redwood-inference/api/pull/1876, https://github.com/redwood-inference/console/pull/542, https://github.com/redwood-inference/sdk-js/pull/210

Ajay Patel: I'll do the final branch pin and tag at 08:45 PST unless someone requests an earlier hold. Command I'll run (for visibility):

```
git checkout release
git merge --no-ff --no-edit origin/main
git tag -a v1.12.0 -m "release: v1.12.0 RC"
git push origin release --tags
```

Maria Chen: Perfect. If runtime tests still flake, we can drop runtime into the first-stage fallback (dedicated v4.1.0) and ship API/console/SDKs. We want to avoid blocking everything for a single kernel tweak.

Nathan Li: Acknowledged — I'll prioritize the softmax precision fix and post a quick patch PR by 05:40 PST if possible. If not, I'll flag runtime for fallback.

Sophia Ruiz: Noted — merging API + console now after final QA ✅

Ben Carter: Updated changelog wording and linked merged PRs: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/333 — ready for final review.

Olivia Park: SDK merged and migration note added. https://github.com/redwood-inference/sdk-js/pull/210 — tagging release lead.

Maria Chen: Thanks everyone. Final reminder: branch freeze 08:30 PST, merge window ends 08:45 PST. If you hit any blockers holler here or @me directly.

release-bot: Reminder scheduled for 2026-03-19 08:20 PST in #eng-releases.

Ajay Patel: I'll post back once I've pinned and pushed the tag (08:45 PST).

Maria Chen: Closing loop — keep updates short here. Good luck!"

title: #eng-releases: 3512345678
preview:
Aisha Khan: deploy-bot paused release-window: preflight reported env parity deltas. Staging: cache ttl=300, kms_alias=alias/redwood-dev, image=redwood/ggml:v3-beta, feature flag 'new-tokenizer'=on. Prod: cache ttl=3600, kms_alias=alias/redwood-prod, image=redwood/ggml:v2.2, flag off.

deploy-bot: preflight checks FAILED: [CACHE_TTL_MISMATCH] s3-cache ttl 300 != 3600; [KMS_ALIAS_DIFF] kms_alias mismatch; [IMAGE_TAG_MISMATCH] container image tag differs; [FEATURE_FLAG_DIFF] new-tokenizer flag mismatch. See /deploy/preflight/3512345678 for details.

Lena Zhou: Oof. We can't flip prod KMS alias to dev alias. infra will veto. Also v3-beta hasn't passed perf benchmarks in prod.

Ben Carter: options: 1) pin canary to prod image (v2.2) and proceed, 2) make staging match prod (ttl + kms + flag) and rerun checks, 3) postpone until infra grants a limited KMS grant (unlikely). I prefer (1) to keep release moving.

Carlos Mendez: +1 to pin. Also add a note about warming the cache after image change, because model artifacts differ and cold misses will increase QPS to S3.

Aisha Khan: agree. I'll prepare a tiny manifest patch to set image -> v2.2 and set the canary feature flag to off. We will not change prod KMS or prod cache TTL in this window. Follow-up ticket required for standardization.

Ben Carter: patch ready in branch: `release/3512345678-pin-image-v2.2`. PR link: https://git.internal/redwood/inference/pull/4123

Aisha Khan: applying PR to canary: `kubectl -n inference set image deploy/infer-worker model=redwood/ggml:v2.2 --record`

deploy-bot: re-running preflight checks... note: image hash for v2.2: `sha256:7b8c9d...`. Warming cache recommended: run job `jobs/cache-warm --model=ggml:v2.2 --workers=5`.

Lena Zhou: infra FYI: INF-4988 created to track KMS alias normalization and cache TTL alignment across envs. Assigning to infra-team for prioritization.

Carlos Mendez: re-run passed ✅ Canary promoted. promotion_id=prom-3512345678.

deploy-bot: release unpaused. All checks green.

Aisha Khan: I'll add a short entry to the release notes: "Canary pinned to prod image v2.2 and feature-flag disabled due to env-parity deltas; follow-up INF-4988 opened for KMS and TTL alignment."

Ben Carter: nice. tagging infra-team to scope INF-4988. :eyes:

Alex Kim: yes. routing rule: if header absent AND client-agent matches legacy list -> route to v1. ops will seed the client-pin rules now.
deploy-bot: Deploy started: service=api-gateway target=canary-wave-1 traffic=5% at 2623456792.

Maria Gonzales: add quality gate: token-level regression >3% on our prompt set triggers an automatic pause.
Ben Carter: adding that to the deploy manifest and release notes.
mobile-team: FYI mobile SDK team will cut release 1.5 in ~2h with header support. we'll backport to 1.4 patch if needed.
Samira Patel: clients we care about: mobile-android <=1.3, mobile-ios <=1.2, embedded SDKs older than 2024-09. list shared in drive.
Alex Kim: if deploy-bot reports >10 distinct client pins within a wave, auto-pause and notify eng-releases. manual override only after triage.
deploy-bot: client-pin-detected: 7 distinct client versions reporting X-Client-Pin=1. Pausing traffic shift at wave=1 at 2623456815. Details: mobile-android:1.3 (4 clients), mobile-ios:1.2 (3 clients).
Alex Kim: pausing rollout. Ops, please roll canary down to 0% and revert any routing changes that sent legacy clients to v2.

deploy-bot: Canary rollback completed at 2623456830. Reason: client pins detected (see above).
Ben Carter: updating release notes with an explicit "paused" banner and instructions for SDK owners + customers.
Maria Gonzales: scheduling 30m sync to decide next steps: wait for SDK 1.5 rollout or widen compatibility shim. I'll ping calendars.
Alex Kim: thanks. outcome options to prepare for the sync: (1) resume staggered rollout after 1.5 release and 24h telemetry, (2) implement temporary routing shim to respect X-Client-Pin indefinitely, (3) full rollback and postpone release. document pros/cons.
Samira Patel: logging action items: ops applied routing pin, mobile-team cutting patch, docs updated, eng-releases on-call notified.
mobile-team: on it — will post PR and timeline in the sync.
Alex Kim: great work. keep the thread updated with deploy-bot outputs and any client escalation requests.

title: #eng-releases: 1796204321
preview:
Ava Chen: Heads up — need to push hotfix v2.3.1 to staging -> prod today, routing policy change included. Can we request a short change-window override?
Ravi Patel: What’s the canary plan? low-latency customers on Dedicated will need careful routing.
Ava Chen: Start 3% for 15m, then 20% for 30m, then ramp to 100%. Rollout config: 
```yaml
service: inference-api
model: text-gen-v2.3.1
steps:
  - canary: 3%
    duration: 15m
  - canary: 20%
    duration: 30m
  - full: 100%
guards:
  - name: latency
    threshold: 200ms
  - name: error_rate
    threshold: 1%
```
Maya Lopez: We’re in a scheduled maintenance window tomorrow morning but not today — why override?
Ava Chen: Customer-facing regression found in routing policy that breaks long-session reconnects. Need immediate rollout to mitigate.
Jordan (oncall): @Maya @Ravi I can take first watch. Ops telemetry shows increased reconnect errors on canary traffic after patch, so we need guard thresholds tuned.
ops-bot: Capacity check: Dedicated pool alpha-2 has 12% spare GPU headroom. Might be tight for a 20% ramp.
deploy-bot: Preflight checks running: lint ok, infra quotas ok, telemetry hooks registered. 3 checks passed, 1 warning (capacity).
Ravi Patel: If capacity is tight, schedule a gradual ramp and enable automatic fallback to v2.3.0 for failing buckets. Add region routing policy to avoid us-west-2 overload.
Ava Chen: Agreed. Update to add fallback and region constraint. Also need approval for a change-window override from Eng Leads.
Maya Lopez: I approve the override for emergency rollout, provided rollback triggers on latency >180ms or error_rate >0.8% for 5m. :thumbsup:
Ravi Patel: I approve with the region constraint and capacity guard. @Jordan keep an eye on console traces.
Jordan (oncall): Will monitor. I'll post telemetry links and add a short eval set to the regression dashboard.
Ava Chen: deploy-bot, schedule rollout now with the updated guards and fallback.

deploy-bot: Scheduled: start in 10m. Preflight summary: capacity warning persists in alpha-2, reduced initial canary to 2% and will auto-pause if CPU > 85% on any host.

Ava Chen: :pray: everyone, thanks for quick approvals.
Maya Lopez: :eyes: good luck.

title: #eng-releases: 1776468110
preview:
Connor O'Brien: Kicking off perf sign-off for Runtime 1.22.0 RC. RC candidate is built from `redwood@b8c3f2e` (tag `runtime:1.22.0-rc3`, image digest `sha256:9d4…`).

Connor O'Brien: Checklist to get to go/no-go today:
1) Benchmark suite v3 run vs 1.21.3 baseline (H100 + A100) - pass/fail + notes
2) Perf canary in 2 regions (low traffic cohorts) - TTFT/TPOT thresholds green
3) Known issues reviewed + mitigations (shape disables, fallbacks) documented
4) CI smoke bench for quant correctness green
5) Evals sign-off for default policy (or confirm defaults remain behind flag)

Connor O'Brien: Please reply here with status/links. Goal: decision by 3pm PT.

ReleaseBot: Release pipeline: `runtime-1.22.0-rc3` promoted to staging. Deploy status: SUCCESS. Change window: 10:00-14:00 PT.

Rafael Mendes: SRE view: canary is running in us-east + eu-central (both on `rc3`). Dashboards here: grafana -> "FP8/INT8 Rollout / Canary" (folder: Serving Runtime). Early signal: us-east green, eu-central had 1 TTFT spike but looks like small sample noise.

Allison Grant: Confirming metrics tagging is in rc3 (PR-19934). We now have `kernel_mode`, `gpu_sku`, `model_id` dimensions in the histos so the canary comparisons aren’t a mess.

Allison Grant: Quick snapshot (last 60m):
- TTFT p50: -3% to -6% (FP8 paths) on H100 cohort
- TPOT p50: +10% tokens/s on decode-heavy workload
- Error rate: flat
- Variance: improved after clock pinning in runner

Hiro Tanaka: Kernel side: FP8 attention integration is in (PR-19827), autoselect heuristics in (PR-19863), plus the KV cache alignment fix (PR-19871). We also have the hotfix disable for the problematic shape regime (PR-19941) included in rc3.

Hiro Tanaka: The shape disable is gated on: (hidden=8192, head_dim=128, seq_len>8k) + A100 only. FP8 still on for H100 in that regime.

Grace Kim: Benchmark matrix update: H100/A100 top 6 models complete as of this morning. Link: Drive -> `fp8-int8-benchmark-matrix-v1` tab "RC3".

Grace Kim: Summary vs 1.21.3:
- H100: +8-15% tokens/s on mixed workloads, TTFT neutral to -4% (better) except tool-calling variant where it’s basically flat.
- A100: +4-9% tokens/s, TTFT neutral; with the shape disable, the prior p95 TTFT regression case is gone.

Sofia Ivanova: Scheduling: rc3 includes the mixed workload knob defaults (PR-19911) + instrumentation (PR-19855). This is important: without the batching window tweak, the kernel wins don’t show up end-to-end.

Sofia Ivanova: Perf note: we’re slightly more aggressive on `prefill_queue_backpressure` now. In our runs it reduced TTFT variance (esp. concurrency>16). No increase in queue drops.

Noah Patel: Perf regression architect check: I reviewed the variance investigation notes (ENG-7429) and the rc3 harness artifacts. The debug logging regression that polluted prior data is NOT present (PR-19918 is in).

Noah Patel: Reproducibility: runner pins driver/CUDA + clocks (benchmark-lab PR-851). For production, we still depend on fleet driver consistency but canary cohorts are currently on the pinned image.

Irene Choi: Fleet sanity: staging/canary nodes in the two regions are on the expected driver (`550.54.14`) + CUDA base (`12.4`). No mismatched cluster left in the canary pool.

Sahana Iyer: CI gates: quantized kernel correctness smoke bench is green on main and on the rc3 branch (PR-19926). It’s still a smoke suite (small shape set) but it caught the INT8 shape guard issue earlier.

Sahana Iyer: I’d like to follow up post-release to expand shape coverage, but no blocker for rc3.

Jonas Weber: Evals sign-off: For 1.22.0 we should NOT flip global defaults to FP8/INT8 for all hosted models. We’re good to ship infra + flags + per-model eligibility.

Jonas Weber: The quick quality sanity suite (eval-harness PR-519) shows:
- INT8: small regressions on summarization prompts for one model family; acceptable only behind opt-in flag + documented caveat
- FP8: long-context regression for one model on A100; the shape disable/fallback mitigates the high-risk case

Jonas Weber: Recommendation: ship with flags default OFF globally; enable via canary profiles per model + GPU.

Connor O'Brien: +1. Release notes already drafted to reflect "available behind flags" (Confluence: runtime-1-22-0-fp8-int8-perf-sprint). Anything else we need to call out as known limitation?

Rafael Mendes: Only operational callout: rollback triggers are wired (perf-canary PR-611 + runtime gating PR-19896). We already had a rollback event earlier this week on a different build; current rc3 didn’t trigger.

Rafael Mendes: Current canary thresholds:
- TTFT p95 regression > 7% for 15m => auto rollback
- TPOT p50 drop > 5% for 30m => rollback
- Error rate +0.2% absolute => rollback
Noise suppression is on (min samples per slice).

Allison Grant: Re: noisy alerts, we adjusted baselines to per-SKU (H100 vs A100) and per mode. That was the main issue.

Grace Kim: One nuance: for decode-only synthetic, INT8 matmul wins big, but in the mixed workload it’s smaller because prefill dominates. We noted that in the matrix so nobody over-reads microbench wins.

Hiro Tanaka: Also: INT8 dispatch guardrails (PR-19841) are strict. If we hit unsupported alignment/shapes it falls back to baseline. That’s expected behavior; not a perf bug.

Connor O'Brien: Can someone paste the exact canary cohort config you used, so release has it in the record?

Rafael Mendes: Yep, from `perf-canary/profiles/runtime-1.22.0-rc3.yaml`:
```yaml
cohorts:
  - name: fp8_int8_rc3_us_east
    region: us-east
    traffic_pct: 2
    models: ["top6"]
    gpu_skus: ["H100", "A100"]
    flags:
      RW_KERNEL_FP8_ATTENTION: "canary"
      RW_KERNEL_INT8_MATMUL: "canary"
```

Connor O'Brien: Checklist to get to go/no-go today:
1) Benchmark suite v3 run vs 1.21.3 baseline (H100 + A100) - pass/fail + notes
2) Perf canary in 2 regions (low traffic cohorts) - TTFT/TPOT thresholds green
3) Known issues reviewed + mitigations (shape disables, fallbacks) documented
4) CI smoke bench for quant correctness green
5) Evals sign-off for default policy (or confirm defaults remain behind flag)

Connor O'Brien: Please reply here with status/links. Goal: decision by 3pm PT.

ReleaseBot: Release pipeline: `runtime-1.22.0-rc3` promoted to staging. Deploy status: SUCCESS. Change window: 10:00-14:00 PT.

Rafael Mendes: SRE view: canary is running in us-east + eu-central (both on `rc3`). Dashboards here: grafana -> "FP8/INT8 Rollout / Canary" (folder: Serving Runtime). Early signal: us-east green, eu-central had 1 TTFT spike but looks like small sample noise.

Allison Grant: Confirming metrics tagging is in rc3 (PR-19934). We now have `kernel_mode`, `gpu_sku`, `model_id` dimensions in the histos so the canary comparisons aren’t a mess.

Allison Grant: Quick snapshot (last 60m):
- TTFT p50: -3% to -6% (FP8 paths) on H100 cohort
- TPOT p50: +10% tokens/s on decode-heavy workload
- Error rate: flat
- Variance: improved after clock pinning in runner

Hiro Tanaka: Kernel side: FP8 attention integration is in (PR-19827), autoselect heuristics in (PR-19863), plus the KV cache alignment fix (PR-19871). We also have the hotfix disable for the problematic shape regime (PR-19941) included in rc3.

Hiro Tanaka: The shape disable is gated on: (hidden=8192, head_dim=128, seq_len>8k) + A100 only. FP8 still on for H100 in that regime.

Grace Kim: Benchmark matrix update: H100/A100 top 6 models complete as of this morning. Link: Drive -> `fp8-int8-benchmark-matrix-v1` tab "RC3".

Grace Kim: Summary vs 1.21.3:
- H100: +8-15% tokens/s on mixed workloads, TTFT neutral to -4% (better) except tool-calling variant where it’s basically flat.
- A100: +4-9% tokens/s, TTFT neutral; with the shape disable, the prior p95 TTFT regression case is gone.

Sofia Ivanova: Scheduling: rc3 includes the mixed workload knob defaults (PR-19911) + instrumentation (PR-19855). This is important: without the batching window tweak, the kernel wins don’t show up end-to-end.

Sofia Ivanova: Perf note: we’re slightly more aggressive on `prefill_queue_backpressure` now. In our runs it reduced TTFT variance (esp. concurrency>16). No increase in queue drops.

Noah Patel: Perf regression architect check: I reviewed the variance investigation notes (ENG-7429) and the rc3 harness artifacts. The debug logging regression that polluted prior data is NOT present (PR-19918 is in).

Noah Patel: Reproducibility: runner pins driver/CUDA + clocks (benchmark-lab PR-851). For production, we still depend on fleet driver consistency but canary cohorts are currently on the pinned image.

Irene Choi: Fleet sanity: staging/canary nodes in the two regions are on the expected driver (`550.54.14`) + CUDA base (`12.4`). No mismatched cluster left in the canary pool.

Sahana Iyer: CI gates: quantized kernel correctness smoke bench is green on main and on the rc3 branch (PR-19926). It’s still a smoke suite (small shape set) but it caught the INT8 shape guard issue earlier.

Sahana Iyer: I’d like to follow up post-release to expand shape coverage, but no blocker for rc3.

Jonas Weber: Evals sign-off: For 1.22.0 we should NOT flip global defaults to FP8/INT8 for all hosted models. We’re good to ship infra + flags + per-model eligibility.

Jonas Weber: The quick quality sanity suite (eval-harness PR-519) shows:
- INT8: small regressions on summarization prompts for one model family; acceptable only behind opt-in flag + documented caveat
- FP8: long-context regression for one model on A100; the shape disable/fallback mitigates the high-risk case

Jonas Weber: Recommendation: ship with flags default OFF globally; enable via canary profiles per model + GPU.

Connor O'Brien: +1. Release notes already drafted to reflect "available behind flags" (Confluence: runtime-1-22-0-fp8-int8-perf-sprint). Anything else we need to call out as known limitation?

Rafael Mendes: Only operational callout: rollback triggers are wired (perf-canary PR-611 + runtime gating PR-19896). We already had a rollback event earlier this week on a different build; current rc3 didn’t trigger.

Rafael Mendes: Current canary thresholds:
- TTFT p95 regression > 7% for 15m => auto rollback
- TPOT p50 drop > 5% for 30m => rollback
- Error rate +0.2% absolute => rollback
Noise suppression is on (min samples per slice).

Allison Grant: Re: noisy alerts, we adjusted baselines to per-SKU (H100 vs A100) and per mode. That was the main issue.

Grace Kim: One nuance: for decode-only synthetic, INT8 matmul wins big, but in the mixed workload it’s smaller because prefill dominates. We noted that in the matrix so nobody over-reads microbench wins.

Hiro Tanaka: Also: INT8 dispatch guardrails (PR-19841) are strict. If we hit unsupported alignment/shapes it falls back to baseline. That’s expected behavior; not a perf bug.

title: #eng-releases: 1772020444
preview:
ReleaseBot: Upcoming release window scheduled: runtime patch `1.18.1` (hotfix). Target start: Tue 2026-02-17 18:00 PT. Rolling by region. Reply here with any doc/runbook/customer-comms dependencies.

Talia Benson: Heads up this patch is tied to a couple of Support escalations (streaming disconnects + rate limit headers). We need docs/known-issues updated so Support isn’t referencing stale guidance during rollout.

Elliot Price: +1. If we’re shipping 1.18.1, we should update:
- Known issues index (mark streaming article as “mitigation improved in 1.18.1”, keep workaround for older runtimes)
- SDK troubleshooting section (streaming reconnect guidance)
- Any internal Support playbook links pointing to the workaround pages

Wei Chen: I can take the Hosted-facing copy updates. Which endpoints/regions are in the canary first?

Mina Haddad: Canary order (Hosted): `us-east` -> `eu-west` -> `us-west`. Dedicated/Private are opt-in / scheduled with account teams. Main behavior change: clearer error classification + request-id propagation on streaming disconnects; also rate-limit headers consistency.

Dev Patel: For Support: we should be explicit about “what changes with 1.18.1” so agents stop asking customers to do heavy client-side tweaks if the fix is already live.

Sean Gallagher: SRE ask: please keep workaround docs framed as “temporary mitigations” and include “when to escalate” (error rate / multiple customers / sustained >X mins). No customer names in any public-facing KB.

Rafael Mendes: Also please make sure the known-issues index notes how Support can confirm whether a customer is hitting fallback routing vs not. During releases we get a lot of “my pinned model was ignored” reports.

Allison Grant: We have a quick-view dashboard link we can safely share internally. If docs want a reference, use the Support-safe set (latency/error/stream health) and avoid linking the raw incident views.

Elliot Price: Current doc PRs in flight:
- `redwood-docs#2841` (known-issues hub + initial workaround set)
- `redwood-docs#2843` (SDK troubleshooting streaming)
We should add a small note to the streaming workaround page: “If runtime >= 1.18.1 and still seeing resets, collect request IDs and escalate.”

Kira Thompson: I’m on the Dedicated/Private side. For the known-issues index entry, do we want a tag that differentiates Hosted runtime vs Private installer/runtime? People keep mixing them.

Dev Patel: Yes please. Support keeps asking “what runtime are you on” and Private customers respond with installer version. If we can add “Hosted runtime” vs “Private runtime/installer” tags it will help triage.

Elliot Price: We can do that in the index taxonomy (tags: `hosted-runtime`, `dedicated-runtime`, `private-installer`). I’ll keep it minimal so it doesn’t blow up the layout.

Wei Chen: Draft wording for streaming article addendum (Hosted):
- “Behavior in 1.18.1: improved disconnect error details + request ID propagation.”
- “Workarounds (client timeouts, retry/backoff, disable HTTP/2) still applicable if you have intermediaries/proxies.”
- “Escalate if: resets persist across networks, multiple regions, or request IDs show server-side disconnect classification.”

Sean Gallagher: Looks fine. Please avoid saying “fixed” unless we’re 100% sure. “Improved” / “mitigation shipped” is safer until we see post-release metrics.

Talia Benson: What’s the merge deadline for docs changes to be live before canary?

Elliot Price: If we want docs updated before Tue 18:00 PT, we need merges by EOD Mon. Docs deploy runs hourly but approvals can be the bottleneck.

Rafael Mendes: +1 to Mon cutoff. Also if there’s any change to incident comms language, make sure it matches the Support comms templates (no promises on exact ETAs).

Dev Patel: Support macro updates will reference the known-issues index + streaming workaround. If we change URLs/tags, please ping me so we don’t ship broken macros.

Kira Thompson: Re: rate limit headers consistency—do we want to update the 429 workaround article to mention “1.18.1 reduces header mismatch scenarios”?

Mina Haddad: Yes, but same caveat: not “fixed” until rollout complete. Also some mismatches can still come from client retries / multiple projects keys. The main change is server classification + consistency across endpoints.

Allison Grant: I’ll drop the internal dashboard link here for Support/Docs references (Support-safe): https://console.redwoodinference.com/ops/dashboards/support-quickview (internal). Please don’t paste into public docs.

Elliot Price: Action items:
1) Wei: update streaming workaround addendum + “runtime >= 1.18.1” note (today)
2) Kira: update 429 workaround copy similarly + add index tags for Hosted vs Private (today)
3) Me: update known-issues index entries w/ “mitigation shipped in 1.18.1” + last-updated dates (tomorrow)
4) Dev: sanity check macro links after docs deploy (Mon)

Wei Chen: Ack. I’ll open a small PR against the streaming workaround page + cross-link from SDK troubleshooting once `2843` merges.

Dev Patel: Thanks. Once merged, I’ll post in #support with the “what changed in 1.18.1” bullets so agents stop giving conflicting guidance.

ReleaseBot: Reminder: if customer-facing behavior changes, add a note to release notes. Reply here with a one-liner summary once finalized.

Elliot Price: Release note proposal:
- “Runtime 1.18.1: improved error details and request ID propagation for streaming disconnects; improved consistency of rate-limit headers across endpoints.”
(avoiding “fixed”)

Sean Gallagher: :thumbsup: from SRE.

Rafael Mendes: :+1: Works.

Talia Benson: Great—let’s keep this thread updated once the doc PRs merge + deploy is confirmed.

title: #eng-releases: 1771227606
preview:
Connor O'Brien: Pulling this thread together for internal release notes + follow-ups after the canary-caught regression yesterday. Goal: write-up for #eng + what we’re doing before we reopen the merge window.

Connor O'Brien: Source incident thread: https://slack.redwood.example/archives/incidents/p1771143201-real-regression-caught-pre-rollout

Incident Bot: (context) Incident 2026-02-15: perf-canary detected sustained p95 regression on top model during limited rollout. Auto-gate triggered. Rollout halted. Reverted runtime build.

Vanessa Ortiz: For release notes: this correlated w/ a fleet change in us-east-a (driver rollout) *and* runtime change. Canary showed both p95 up + tokens/sec down.

Noah Patel: Canary signal details (for notes):
- model: `redwood/llama-3-70b-instruct` (prod alias)
- region: us-east
- hw: H100-80GB
- observed: p95 e2e +18-22% (runtime-only +15%), tokens/sec -9-11%
- queueing time flat -> likely execution path
- kernel_tag changed due to auto-selection tweak in runtime build `rt-2026.02.15.3`

Connor O'Brien: Great. We should keep it high level for release notes but include links to dashboard/graphs.

Aisha Bello: Dashboard link for the write-up: https://grafana.redwood.example/d/perf-canary-model?var-model=llama-3-70b&var-region=us-east
(Panel to call out: p95 runtime-only + kernel tag overlay)

Rafael Mendes: From on-call side: paging did not fire (this was within phase-1 gates, not paging-enabled). Gate pause did exactly what we want. Mention that explicitly.

Allison Grant: +1 and please state “no customer impact” (limited rollout, gate blocked). Also note we’re keeping warning rules on but still tuning paging thresholds.

DeployBot: Rollback executed by @Connor O'Brien
- Service: redwood-runtime
- Region: us-east
- From: rt-2026.02.15.3
- To: rt-2026.02.12.1
- Status: SUCCESS

Mateo Alvarez: For the internal note: highlight that the canary prevented broad impact and that attribution was fast because of kernel tagging + queueing split. This is the story we want.

Hiro Tanaka: Root cause (tentative): kernel auto-selection picked a slower fused attention variant for the long-prefill bucket. Triggered by a heuristic change in 02.15.3. Fix is to pin previous kernel for that bucket on H100 until we re-benchmark.

Sofia Ivanova: Scheduler wasn’t the culprit (batch size dist unchanged, queue time stable). Worth calling out so people don’t chase the wrong thing next time.

Logan Wright: Telemetry note: we saw ingestion lag ~3-4 min in the middle of the event. Didn’t affect gate decision but made graphs confusing. I’ll add an action item to alert on pipeline lag for canary metrics.

Connor O'Brien: Draft release note bullets (please nitpick):
1) 02/15 limited runtime rollout paused by perf-canary gate due to sustained perf regression on a top model (p95 latency up, tokens/sec down).
2) No customer impact; rollout halted before expansion.
3) Rapid attribution via runtime-only latency + kernel_tag; scheduler/queueing signals were stable.
4) Mitigation: rollback to previous runtime build; kernel heuristic change reverted/pinned pending benchmark validation.
5) Follow-ups: tighten gate criteria + add additional prefill/decode breakdown panels; track telemetry pipeline lag.

Marissa Cole: Add one line on rollout workflow: “Gates set to auto-pause expansion; manual approval required to resume.” Product wants that documented as expected behavior.

Paula Mendes: Also, can we add “what changed” in the release itself? i.e., “kernel auto-selection heuristic update” (without too much detail). Useful for audits.

Sean Gallagher: From SRE: +1 on explicitly noting this did *not* page. We should avoid setting the expectation that every gate event == on-call incident.

Quentin Harris: Model registry piece: was model alias stable? For notes, mention we were pinned to version `llama3-70b-instruct@2026-02-10` so it wasn’t a model rollout.

Noah Patel: Confirmed. Model version didn’t move. Canary baseline was keyed on model+version+hw+rt_build.

Connor O'Brien: Good catch, adding: “model version unchanged; regression isolated to runtime build change.”

Allison Grant: Action items should be concrete with owners. Suggest:
- (Hiro) revert/pin kernel heuristic
- (Noah) add gate rule to require N samples per bucket before triggering
- (Logan) telemetry lag alert
- (Connor) update release checklist to include “canary green for 60 min” before region expansion

Vanessa Ortiz: Also add one for fleet signals: we should surface “driver version” tag on the canary dashboard. Even if not RCA here, it’s a common confounder.

Logan Wright: I can help but that’s probably Fleet Telemetry. Vanessa can you take an owner for that and I’ll pair?

Vanessa Ortiz: Yep. I’ll open a ticket for driver tag -> runtime telemetry (or at least the canary label set) and coordinate w/ Omar.

Rafael Mendes: For completeness: runbook link we used during triage: https://confluence.redwood.example/display/SRE/Runbook+-+Perf+Regression+Canary+Alert
This should go in the notes as “triage path”.

Connor O'Brien: Ok I’ll post a short internal note to #eng + attach to the next release change log. Also updating the eng-releases checklist doc.

Mateo Alvarez: Please include a brief “what worked / what was confusing” section. Confusing bits: telemetry lag, and initial question “fleet vs runtime”. What worked: canary gate + kernel tags.

Chloe Martin: If you want this visible in Console release history, send me the final bullet list and I’ll add it to the internal-only “Ops Notes” section.

Connor O'Brien: Will do. Targeting EOD.

Paula Mendes: One more request: explicitly state criteria for resuming rollout (e.g., fix merged + staged perf-canary validation + 24h warning-only). Helps align stakeholders.

Connor O'Brien: Added as final bullet: “Resume requires (a) kernel pin fix shipped, (b) staging validation plan signed off, (c) perf-canary green 60 min in us-east phase-1, (d) SRE + runtime approval to expand.”

Aisha Bello: Works for me. :thumbsup:

Hiro Tanaka: :thumbsup: I’ll post an update once the pin is in and benchmarks rerun.

Connor O'Brien: Thanks all. I’ll circulate draft in this thread before posting to #eng.

Hiro Tanaka: Root cause (tentative): kernel auto-selection picked a slower fused attention variant for the long-prefill bucket. Triggered by a heuristic change in 02.15.3. Fix is to pin previous kernel for that bucket on H100 until we re-benchmark.

Sofia Ivanova: Scheduler wasn’t the culprit (batch size dist unchanged, queue time stable). Worth calling out so people don’t chase the wrong thing next time.

Logan Wright: Telemetry note: we saw ingestion lag ~3-4 min in the middle of the event. Didn’t affect gate decision but made graphs confusing. I’ll add an action item to alert on pipeline lag for canary metrics.

Connor O'Brien: Draft release note bullets (please nitpick):
1) 02/15 limited runtime rollout paused by perf-canary gate due to sustained perf regression on a top model (p95 latency up, tokens/sec down).
2) No customer impact; rollout halted before expansion.
3) Rapid attribution via runtime-only latency + kernel_tag; scheduler/queueing signals were stable.
4) Mitigation: rollback to previous runtime build; kernel heuristic change reverted/pinned pending benchmark validation.
5) Follow-ups: tighten gate criteria + add additional prefill/decode breakdown panels; track telemetry pipeline lag.

Marissa Cole: Add one line on rollout workflow: “Gates set to auto-pause expansion; manual approval required to resume.” Product wants that documented as expected behavior.

Paula Mendes: Also, can we add “what changed” in the release itself? i.e., “kernel auto-selection heuristic update” (without too much detail). Useful for audits.

Sean Gallagher: From SRE: +1 on explicitly noting this did *not* page. We should avoid setting the expectation that every gate event == on-call incident.

Quentin Harris: Model registry piece: was model alias stable? For notes, mention we were pinned to version `llama3-70b-instruct@2026-02-10` so it wasn’t a model rollout.

Noah Patel: Confirmed. Model version didn’t move. Canary baseline was keyed on model+version+hw+rt_build.

Connor O'Brien: Good catch, adding: “model version unchanged; regression isolated to runtime build change.”

Allison Grant: Action items should be concrete with owners. Suggest:
- (Hiro) revert/pin kernel heuristic
- (Noah) add gate rule to require N samples per bucket before triggering
- (Logan) telemetry lag alert
- (Connor) update release checklist to include “canary green for 60 min” before region expansion

Vanessa Ortiz: Also add one for fleet signals: we should surface “driver version” tag on the canary dashboard. Even if not RCA here, it’s a common confounder.

Logan Wright: I can help but that’s probably Fleet Telemetry. Vanessa can you take an owner for that and I’ll pair?

Vanessa Ortiz: Yep. I’ll open a ticket for driver tag -> runtime telemetry (or at least the canary label set) and coordinate w/ Omar.

Rafael Mendes: For completeness: runbook link we used during triage: https://confluence.redwood.example/display/SRE/Runbook+-+Perf+Regression+Canary+Alert
This should go in the notes as “triage path”.

Connor O'Brien: Ok I’ll post a short internal note to #eng + attach to the next release change log. Also updating the eng-releases checklist doc.

Mateo Alvarez: Please include a brief “what worked / what was confusing” section. Confusing bits: telemetry lag, and initial question “fleet vs runtime”. What worked: canary gate + kernel tags.

Chloe Martin: If you want this visible in Console release history, send me the final bullet list and I’ll add it to the internal-only “Ops Notes” section.

Connor O'Brien: Will do. Targeting EOD.

Paula Mendes: One more request: explicitly state criteria for resuming rollout (e.g., fix merged + staged perf-canary validation + 24h warning-only). Helps align stakeholders.

Connor O'Brien: Added as final bullet: “Resume requires (a) kernel pin fix shipped, (b) staging validation plan signed off, (c) perf-canary green 60 min in us-east phase-1, (d) SRE + runtime approval to expand.”

Aisha Bello: Works for me. :thumbsup:

Hiro Tanaka: :thumbsup: I’ll post an update once the pin is in and benchmarks rerun.

Connor O'Brien: Thanks all. I’ll circulate draft in this thread before posting to #eng.

title: #eng-releases: 1770394481
preview:
Connor O'Brien: heads up, release train next week includes the auto-rollback-on-SLO-breach bits behind flags. want to lock staged enablement + checklist today so we don't wing it in prod.

Talia Benson: +1. can you drop the proposed phase plan here? i'll mirror it in ENG-4850.

Connor O'Brien: proposal (rough):
1) dev + staging: flag ON by default, all internal routes ok
2) prod: flag OFF globally, enable allowlist for 1 internal route in us-east only
3) expand to 2nd region + 1 hosted tier route
4) dedicated/private opt-in only (support-driven) until we have 2 weeks clean

Connor O'Brien: key question: do we have 1 or 2 flags? (eval logic vs action execution)

Jada Williams: controller side we have `rollouts.slo_eval_enabled` and `rollouts.auto_action_enabled`. eval can run + emit audit events even if action disabled.

Benji Okafor: policy engine schema is going out w/ defaults but we can set `action_mode=pause_only` for the canary phase even if `auto_action_enabled` is true. safer.

Sean Gallagher: from SRE POV: want eval on early so we can validate monitor wiring + staleness handling. but action should be *pause-only* first. rollback execution adds more risk.

Rafael Mendes: agree. also need a very explicit backout: one click = flip flag off + ignore policy actions. and document what happens to rollouts already paused.

Connor O'Brien: ok so staged plan becomes:
- Phase A (staging): eval ON + action ON but `pause_only` default template
- Phase B (prod internal canary): eval ON for allowlisted scope; action ON + pause_only
- Phase C (prod internal canary+): enable rollback execution for allowlisted scope

Aisha Bello: for Phase B/C, alerting must be wired *before* enablement. we have new rules in observability-pack PR-913 but need to confirm routing. also: please ensure no paging storm (notify vs page).

Leila Farouk: staging E2Es can validate pause + rollback. but if prod phase is pause-only first, we should add an explicit E2E to confirm that rollback does *not* happen when policy says pause_only.

Hannah Rivera: Console UX: we show the auto action timeline either way (pause/rollback/suppressed). if eval is on but action is off, we'll show outcome = "would_have_rolled_back"? or "suppressed_by_flag".

Jada Williams: we already emit decision outcome `suppressed` with reason `feature_flag_disabled` (matches ADR-0128 draft). so Console can show it.

Connor O'Brien: perfect. release checklist items i'm tracking:
- [ ] flags defined + default states documented
- [ ] allowlist mechanism (route/model/region/tier) reviewed
- [ ] backout procedure tested (flip flags, confirm controller stops actions)
- [ ] runbook link + oncall training scheduled
- [ ] dashboards + alerts deployed + access confirmed
- [ ] required monitors present gate (release gate)
- [ ] rollback executor idempotency validated

Sean Gallagher: add: "monitor missing/stale" behavior decision (fail-open vs fail-closed) must be consistent with runbook. my pref: fail-closed for rollback (i.e., don’t rollback if data is stale), but still allow pause? we debated this.

Benji Okafor: current logic: if monitor stale/missing -> no action + emit `suppressed(stale_monitor)`; rollout continues. that’s effectively fail-open. we can change per action type but that’s extra complexity.

Rafael Mendes: if we continue on missing monitor, we need a *loud* alert. otherwise people think auto-rollback is protecting them when it isn’t.

Aisha Bello: we have alert `rollout_slo_monitor_stale` -> notify #eng-sre + service owner channel (non-paging). paging only when we actually take rollback action (and only for tier >= dedicated).

Connor O'Brien: noted. i'll add checklist item: "stale monitor alert rule live".

Talia Benson: can we agree on flag names and owner? release notes need the exact strings.

Jada Williams: controller flags:
- `rollouts.slo_eval_enabled` (owner: eng-platform)
- `rollouts.auto_action_enabled` (owner: eng-platform)
- plus config: `rollouts.auto_action_max_per_hour` guardrail

Benji Okafor: policy side has `autoRollback` section. for MVP templates: `PauseOnFastBurn` and `RollbackOnFastBurn`.

Connor O'Brien: rollout plan detail (prod):
- day 0: ship code w/ flags OFF
- day 1: enable eval for internal canary route `api/chat` model `rw-llama-70b-v4` in `us-east-1` for `tier=internal`
- day 2: enable action pause-only same scope
- day 4: enable rollback execution same scope
- week 2: add `eu-west-1`
- week 3: allow hosted self-serve opt-in (beta)

actionable?

Sean Gallagher: i'd move rollback execution to day 3 only if game day passes in staging this week. otherwise keep it day 7. we need at least one simulated incident run w/ real alert routing.

Leila Farouk: staging game day is on the calendar for Thu. we can run 3 scenarios: latency fast burn, 5xx spike, stale monitor. i’ll post results.

Rafael Mendes: +1. also require runbook finalized before enabling rollback action in prod. pause-only is ok earlier (lower blast radius).

Connor O'Brien: ok: prod rollback action gated on:
- game day complete + signoff from SRE (Sean/Rafael)
- runbook published
- dashboards accessible to oncall group

Hannah Rivera: also gating on Console surfaces? if we take action and nobody can see "why" it'll be rough.

Connor O'Brien: good call. add: Console event detail panel shipped (at least minimal) OR link out to audit log + dashboard.

release-bot: Reminder: Release freeze begins in 3 days. Please tag release-critical PRs with `release-train`.

Benji Okafor: policy schema PR is tagged. validation errors are decent but we should add one more: if someone sets threshold < safe min, we hard fail and show message (see Slack thread in eng-platform). will that block train?

Connor O'Brien: if it's low risk + behind flag, we can ship and patch, but i’d prefer at least the safe bounds in before prod canary. don’t block on better error copy.

Aisha Bello: one more: alert routing config should *not* page primary oncall for self-serve tier. notify only. can we encode that in labels? (tier label present?)

Jada Williams: metrics include `customer_tier` label but we’re being careful about cardinality. it's one of a fixed enum (internal/self_serve/dedicated/private). safe.

Sean Gallagher: ok.

Connor O'Brien: decisions summary (unless objections):
- ship code with flags OFF in prod
- enable eval first (allowlisted), then pause-only actions
- rollback execution requires game day + SRE signoff + runbook + dashboards access
- stale/missing monitor -> suppress action + emit event + alert (notify)
- clear backout: flip `rollouts.auto_action_enabled` OFF globally (and optionally eval off)

Rafael Mendes: 👍

Leila Farouk: 👍 will post game day notes here.

Talia Benson: i’ll update ENG-4850 w/ the gating checklist + phases.

Connor O'Brien: cool. i'll send release notes email w/ flags + staged schedule and link to runbook when ready.

title: #eng-releases: 1769462507
preview:
Connor O'Brien: looking to lock next week’s release train (Wed). can we include the cost model changes? I’m tracking:
- pr-28451 add cost-model schema package v1
- pr-28457 cost-model service internal endpoints
- pr-28462 client library (version negotiation)
plus the console switch pr-28470 is “optional” depending on risk.

Connor O'Brien: need owners to confirm (1) rollout/flagging (2) rollback plan (3) any migration/backfill required. we had a couple last-minute schema changes in other trains, so trying to avoid surprises.

Talia Benson: +1. we should be explicit on what’s going out vs what stays dark. success criteria for train should be “no customer-visible changes, no billing diffs”.

Ethan Park: from platform side: schema package + service endpoints are safe to ship. endpoints are internal-only, behind auth. no existing callers except the staging canary jobs.
Ethan Park: console integration (pr-28470) is gated behind `console_cost_model_api_enabled` and has fallback to existing aggregation path.

Chloe Martin: for Console we can hold pr-28470 if release wants to be conservative. but we’d like schema+service to land so we can start dogfooding in staging.

Jada Williams: what’s the default behavior if the cost-model service errors? (timeouts, 5xx) is it hard-fail on the request path? we’re sensitive to latency spikes.

Ethan Park: client lib defaults:
- timeout 150ms (configurable)
- retries 1 (only on safe idempotent endpoints)
- on timeout/error, console caller should fall back (we already do in pr-28470)
Also server side has request bounds, and we’ve got caching on the “estimate” path for common shapes.

Rafael Mendes: release question from SRE: are we introducing any new mandatory dependencies for Console prod traffic this train? if pr-28470 is included, it *is* a new dependency (even with fallback) and will show up in latency graphs.

Connor O'Brien: that’s what I need to decide. schema+service alone seems fine. console adoption is the risk.

Allison Grant: from observability POV, if you do ship pr-28470, please ensure it emits:
- feature flag state
- fallback reason counts
- cost_model_api latency (p50/p95) as a separate timer
so we can quickly tell if the new path is the culprit.

Chloe Martin: yep, metrics are in the PR:
`console.cost_model_api.enabled` (gauge)
`console.cost_model_api.fallback.count` (tagged by reason)
`console.cost_model_api.request_ms`

Kaitlyn Nguyen: auth/tenancy note: cost-model service checks `tenant_id` from mTLS principal + request header match. if mismatch -> 403. no “best effort” there.
Kaitlyn Nguyen: for rollback, worst case we turn off the console flag and traffic goes back to old path. service can stay up.

Cole Summers: billing hooks not part of this train, right? (pr-28521 later). just want to avoid “numbers changed” escalations.

title: #eng-releases: 1769118802
preview:
Connor O'Brien: Requesting a temporary release freeze for serving runtime rollouts until perf-canary signals stabilize + we finish threshold tuning. We had 2 warning spikes yesterday + 1 near-page this AM that look like baseline drift / ingestion lag, not real perf.

Connor O'Brien: Proposal:
- Freeze: runtime + kernel rollouts to prod (hosted + dedicated) for 48h
- Allowed: Sev1/Sev2 fixes w/ explicit signoff from SRE + runtime TL
- Goal: reduce overlapping changes while we tune min-samples/dampening + region/SKU thresholds

Connor O'Brien: Context links:
- Incident thread: https://slack.redwood.example/archives/incidents/p1769034455
- Canary baseline drift discussion: https://slack.redwood.example/archives/eng-runtime/p1768604412
- Current rollout schedule sheet: https://drive.redwood.example/eng-releases/runtime-rollouts

Noah Patel: +1 from runtime. The canary regression evaluator is too twitchy in us-east right now. We’re pushing a config change today to bump min sample size and add a 15m hold before escalating warning->page.

Allison Grant: To be clear, are we freezing only the runtime deploy train, or also telemetry/schema changes? Some of the noise is from metrics pipeline latency, so freezing runtime alone might not help.

Connor O'Brien: Good q. My intent is specifically changes that can affect latency/tps (runtime build, kernels, scheduler config, fleet placement). Telemetry/schema changes can proceed but please avoid breaking dashboards/alerts.

Sean Gallagher: SRE vote: freeze is reasonable. Oncall got a bunch of low-signal noise this week and it’s hard to tell what’s real vs baseline wobble. We should also lock the canary target model list during the freeze.

Vanessa Ortiz: Fleet side: we have a planned GPU driver patch in eu-west tonight. That could move perf a bit. Do we want to pause that too? It’s “infra” but impacts canary.

Connor O'Brien: If it moves perf and canary can’t attribute yet, we should defer unless it’s urgent. Can you check if we can slip 48h?

Vanessa Ortiz: I can slip it to Friday. Not urgent.

Hiro Tanaka: Kernel team had a minor kernel selection tweak queued (auto-select fallback ordering). Not merged yet. Happy to hold.

Sofia Ivanova: Scheduler change (batching heuristic) is in staging only; was tentatively going to prod Thu. We can pause. I’d prefer the freeze includes scheduler flags too (we’ve seen queueing-time shift look like latency regression).

Logan Wright: Re: telemetry pipeline, we’re debugging ingestion delay in us-east. Worst-case 8–12m lag during peak. That made “observed window” mismatched vs baseline. We have a fix behind a feature flag and can roll it safely (it’s just buffering/flush). Not performance-impacting for inference.

Allison Grant: +1 to allow telemetry fixes. But we should call out: any telemetry change needs to keep label cardinality stable. Last week we almost exploded series count.

Rafael Mendes: From paging hygiene side: thank you. The near-page this AM was painful—looked like p95 +18% on a top model, but trace links were empty b/c metrics were late. Freeze helps us not chase ghosts.

Marissa Cole: Product perspective: ok with a 48h freeze as long as we keep the “break glass” path for real regressions. Also can we use this time to align what canary gates should block? Right now it’s unclear if warning blocks rollout or only paging.

Connor O'Brien: Agree. During freeze, I’ll draft a simple gating rule for Phase 1: warning does NOT auto-block, sustained paging DOES block + triggers rollback recommendation. We’ll iterate.

Talia Benson: Can someone state the exact start/end times and what’s included? I’ll update the release calendar + send a note.

Connor O'Brien: Start: now (post this thread). End: 48h from now (or earlier if Noah/Sean sign off that canary is stable). Included:
- Runtime build rollouts (any region/tier)
- Kernel changes / kernel selector behavior changes
- Scheduler config/flags that impact batching/queueing
- Fleet perf-impacting changes (driver, GPU SKU swap) unless urgent
Excluded:
- Telemetry pipeline fixes (w/ review)
- Console/dashboard updates

ReleaseBot: :rotating_light: Release freeze marker added to calendar: “Runtime Perf Canary Stabilization Freeze” (owner: Connor O'Brien).

Noah Patel: Plan for unfreeze criteria:
1) Min-samples + dampening config deployed
2) At least 24h without false positive warnings on the initial canary model set in us-east
3) Telemetry ingestion lag <2m p95 (Logan owns)

Sean Gallagher: +1. Also add: if we do get a real regression during freeze, we can still rollback/disable risky flags. Freeze is about forward rollouts.

Vanessa Ortiz: Confirmed: eu-west driver patch moved to Friday. I’ll note it on the infra change log.

Logan Wright: I’ll push telemetry lag fix today. Will post before/after graphs in #eng-platform.

Allison Grant: Please ensure canary alert payloads include “data freshness” / ingestion delay so oncall can quickly see when it’s a pipeline artifact.

Noah Patel: We can add a “metrics_lag_seconds” field to the incident-bot payload quickly (or at least in the runbook). I’ll sync with Caleb.

Mateo Alvarez: Approving freeze. Thanks for making this explicit—too many moving parts right now. Keep it tight (48h) and use it to get canary signal quality up.

Connor O'Brien: Thanks all. Action items:
- (Noah) deploy min-samples+dampening config today; lock canary model list for 48h
- (Logan) telemetry lag fix + report p95 lag
- (Vanessa) hold eu-west driver change until Friday
- (Connor) update release calendar + send note + draft gating rule for Phase 1

Talia Benson: I’ll send the note to eng-runtime/eng-sre/eng-platform and update the weekly release email. Please @me if anything should be exempted explicitly.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: Oncall will be grateful. If we still see noise after the config changes, we should consider lowering alert sensitivity or adding a longer confirmation window before paging.

Connor O'Brien: Ack. If we’re not stable by tomorrow afternoon, we’ll extend freeze or narrow paging scope to top-3 only until we tune per-region thresholds.

title: #eng-releases: 1767911442
preview:
Connor O'Brien: hey folks — need to lock down what’s going into runtime-1.22 re: KV compaction. Trying to avoid “big risky PRs land Friday, branch cut Monday” :grimacing:

Connor O'Brien: questions:
1) are we carrying compaction in 1.22 or deferring to 1.23?
2) what’s the default state of the feature flag(s) on day-1 of release?
3) what’s our rollback story if p99 spikes after the train goes out?

Talia Benson: +1, also need a single sentence for release notes like “present but disabled by default” vs “enabled for hosted canary only”.

Omar El-Sayed: we’re planning to carry in 1.22, but *flagged*. goal is to get allocator + scheduler hooks in, default OFF globally, then progressively enable via config.

Samir Qureshi: for “what lands”: would like these PRs in 1.22:
- pr-19342 (metrics) ✅ already in main
- pr-19357 (core allocator changes + relocation primitives) behind `kv_compaction_enabled`
- pr-19371 (scheduler budget enforcement) behind same gate
- pr-19422 (kill-switch/config plumbing) so oncall can disable quickly

Samir Qureshi: eviction (pr-19388) is nice-to-have but more behavioral risk. we can keep it disabled (or not ship it) and still get fragmentation improvements via compaction alone.

Sofia Ivanova: +1 on keeping eviction separate. scheduler hook is the bigger safety net (budgeting at prefill/decode boundaries) and reduces “mystery stalls”.

Connor O'Brien: ok. my bias is: ship the plumbing + metrics + compaction core if fully gated, but avoid “partial gating” where code paths run w/o being obvious.

Benji Okafor: on flags: we can do a single top-level gate plus sub-flags.
- `kv_compaction_enabled` (master)
- `kv_compaction_budget_us` / `kv_compaction_budget_bytes`
- `kv_compaction_frag_threshold`
- `kv_eviction_enabled`

Benji Okafor: defaults in 1.22 config package can be master=OFF, eviction=OFF. budget defaults present but inert. then Hosted can flip master ON for canary cohorts via rollout config.

Sean Gallagher: from SRE perspective: we need “OFF by default” for everyone except internal canary, at least for first train. we can’t ship with “surprise ON” anywhere.

Allison Grant: also please ensure metrics emit even when disabled (or at least allocator fragmentation ratio) so we can compare before/after when we enable.

Omar El-Sayed: metrics are already unconditional (fragmentation ratio, arena stats). compaction-specific metrics (bytes moved, compaction time) will be 0 when disabled.

Rafael Mendes: on rollback story: we need kill-switch that can be flipped from control plane config w/o restart. also need documented “what to check” for oncall.

Benji Okafor: pr-19422 adds config plumbing so we can flip at runtime; propagation is ~30–60s for Hosted, longer for Dedicated depending on customer config sync. but still much better than “revert + redeploy”.

Connor O'Brien: timeline check — runtime-1.22 branch cut is next week. what’s the landing risk for pr-19357/pr-19371? those are not small.

Samir Qureshi: pr-19357 is the biggest diff. it’s behind the gate but touches allocator internals. we’ve got unit tests + stress tests in progress. would prefer to land no later than EOD tomorrow to get weekend soak.

Sofia Ivanova: pr-19371 is smaller and depends on 19357. I can land it right after 19357, but if 19357 slips, 19371 slips.

Talia Benson: can we commit to “stop landing new compaction code after branch cut” and only do backports if there’s a must-fix correctness issue?

Connor O'Brien: yes. for release stability: if these miss the cut, we don’t cram them.

Omar El-Sayed: agreed. compaction in 1.22 is “present but disabled by default”; enablement is a post-release config rollout.

Sean Gallagher: can we explicitly state: master flag OFF for Hosted + Dedicated + Private by default in 1.22 artifacts.

Benji Okafor: yep. I’ll set defaults in the runtime config bundle to OFF. rollout system will be the only thing turning it ON (and only for canary).

Allison Grant: please also add guardrail: if compaction time exceeds budget or bytes moved spikes, it should self-throttle. (I know that’s in design, just want it called out for release signoff.)

Samir Qureshi: already in implementation: budget enforced per scheduler tick; compaction does at most N micro-steps and yields.

Connor O'Brien: what about eviction? decision for 1.22: ship code but keep OFF? or exclude entirely?

Omar El-Sayed: preference: if eviction PR isn’t merged & stabilized by cut, we exclude. if it merges early enough, we can carry it but leave `kv_eviction_enabled=OFF` everywhere. either way, no runtime behavior change unless master ON.

Rafael Mendes: please don’t carry eviction if it increases risk of correctness bugs even when disabled (e.g., code paths compiled in but not executed should be safe, but we’ve been burned by “disabled” code still affecting state).

Sofia Ivanova: eviction touches selection logic + pinned sessions. even if off, the extra state tracking might exist. I’d vote exclude for this train unless we have a compelling reason.

Connor O'Brien: ok — release call: runtime-1.22 includes metrics + config plumbing + compaction core + scheduler budget hook. eviction is excluded from 1.22 unless Omar/Samir say it’s zero-risk and fully baked by tomorrow.

Talia Benson: :thumbsup: I’ll update the release plan ticket w/ that statement.

ReleaseBot: runtime-1.22 branch cut reminder: freeze begins in ~4 days. Please have high-risk PRs merged and soaked before freeze.

Connor O'Brien: action items:
- @Samir land pr-19357 by EOD tomorrow or call it out if slipping
- @Sofia follow w/ pr-19371 after
- @Benji ensure defaults OFF in 1.22 config + confirm kill-switch works w/o restart
- @Rafael + @Sean confirm rollback authority + comms (who flips kill-switch)

Sean Gallagher: SRE side: rollback authority during canary = oncall SRE can flip kill-switch, notify runtime in #eng-oncall. after-hours = same.

Rafael Mendes: +1, but runbook needs explicit “symptoms” (p99 spike + bytes_moved correlation) to avoid flapping.

Allison Grant: I’ll add a note to the metrics standard doc for “signals that indicate compaction-caused latency” (bytes_moved burst + compaction_time_us + p99).

Omar El-Sayed: thanks. will keep eviction out unless we magically finish it early + soak. otherwise target 1.22.x follow-on.

Connor O'Brien: perfect. ping this thread when 19357 is merged so I can mark the train risk as “contained by flag + kill-switch”.

Talia Benson: +1, also need a single sentence for release notes like “present but disabled by default” vs “enabled for hosted canary only”.

Omar El-Sayed: we’re planning to carry in 1.22, but *flagged*. goal is to get allocator + scheduler hooks in, default OFF globally, then progressively enable via config.

Samir Qureshi: for “what lands”: would like these PRs in 1.22:
- pr-19342 (metrics) ✅ already in main
- pr-19357 (core allocator changes + relocation primitives) behind `kv_compaction_enabled`
- pr-19371 (scheduler budget enforcement) behind same gate
- pr-19422 (kill-switch/config plumbing) so oncall can disable quickly

Samir Qureshi: eviction (pr-19388) is nice-to-have but more behavioral risk. we can keep it disabled (or not ship it) and still get fragmentation improvements via compaction alone.

Sofia Ivanova: +1 on keeping eviction separate. scheduler hook is the bigger safety net (budgeting at prefill/decode boundaries) and reduces “mystery stalls”.

Connor O'Brien: ok. my bias is: ship the plumbing + metrics + compaction core if fully gated, but avoid “partial gating” where code paths run w/o being obvious.

Benji Okafor: on flags: we can do a single top-level gate plus sub-flags.
- `kv_compaction_enabled` (master)
- `kv_compaction_budget_us` / `kv_compaction_budget_bytes`
- `kv_compaction_frag_threshold`
- `kv_eviction_enabled`

Benji Okafor: defaults in 1.22 config package can be master=OFF, eviction=OFF. budget defaults present but inert. then Hosted can flip master ON for canary cohorts via rollout config.

Sean Gallagher: from SRE perspective: we need “OFF by default” for everyone except internal canary, at least for first train. we can’t ship with “surprise ON” anywhere.

Allison Grant: also please ensure metrics emit even when disabled (or at least allocator fragmentation ratio) so we can compare before/after when we enable.

Omar El-Sayed: metrics are already unconditional (fragmentation ratio, arena stats). compaction-specific metrics (bytes moved, compaction time) will be 0 when disabled.

Rafael Mendes: on rollback story: we need kill-switch that can be flipped from control plane config w/o restart. also need documented “what to check” for oncall.

Benji Okafor: pr-19422 adds config plumbing so we can flip at runtime; propagation is ~30–60s for Hosted, longer for Dedicated depending on customer config sync. but still much better than “revert + redeploy”.

Connor O'Brien: timeline check — runtime-1.22 branch cut is next week. what’s the landing risk for pr-19357/pr-19371? those are not small.

Samir Qureshi: pr-19357 is the biggest diff. it’s behind the gate but touches allocator internals. we’ve got unit tests + stress tests in progress. would prefer to land no later than EOD tomorrow to get weekend soak.

Sofia Ivanova: pr-19371 is smaller and depends on 19357. I can land it right after 19357, but if 19357 slips, 19371 slips.

Talia Benson: can we commit to “stop landing new compaction code after branch cut” and only do backports if there’s a must-fix correctness issue?

Connor O'Brien: yes. for release stability: if these miss the cut, we don’t cram them.

Omar El-Sayed: agreed. compaction in 1.22 is “present but disabled by default”; enablement is a post-release config rollout.

Sean Gallagher: can we explicitly state: master flag OFF for Hosted + Dedicated + Private by default in 1.22 artifacts.

Benji Okafor: yep. I’ll set defaults in the runtime config bundle to OFF. rollout system will be the only thing turning it ON (and only for canary).

Allison Grant: please also add guardrail: if compaction time exceeds budget or bytes moved spikes, it should self-throttle. (I know that’s in design, just want it called out for release signoff.)

Samir Qureshi: already in implementation: budget enforced per scheduler tick; compaction does at most N micro-steps and yields.

Connor O'Brien: what about eviction? decision for 1.22: ship code but keep OFF? or exclude entirely?

Omar El-Sayed: preference: if eviction PR isn’t merged & stabilized by cut, we exclude. if it merges early enough, we can carry it but leave `kv_eviction_enabled=OFF` everywhere. either way, no runtime behavior change unless master ON.

Rafael Mendes: please don’t carry eviction if it increases risk of correctness bugs even when disabled (e.g., code paths compiled in but not executed should be safe, but we’ve been burned by “disabled” code still affecting state).

Sofia Ivanova: eviction touches selection logic + pinned sessions. even if off, the extra state tracking might exist. I’d vote exclude for this train unless we have a compelling reason.

Connor O'Brien: ok — release call: runtime-1.22 includes metrics + config plumbing + compaction core + scheduler budget hook. eviction is excluded from 1.22 unless Omar/Samir say it’s zero-risk and fully baked by tomorrow.

Talia Benson: :thumbsup: I’ll update the release plan ticket w/ that statement.

ReleaseBot: runtime-1.22 branch cut reminder: freeze begins in ~4 days. Please have high-risk PRs merged and soaked before freeze.

Connor O'Brien: action items:
- @Samir land pr-19357 by EOD tomorrow or call it out if slipping
- @Sofia follow w/ pr-19371 after
- @Benji ensure defaults OFF in 1.22 config + confirm kill-switch works w/o restart
- @Rafael + @Sean confirm rollback authority + comms (who flips kill-switch)

Sean Gallagher: SRE side: rollback authority during canary = oncall SRE can flip kill-switch, notify runtime in #eng-oncall. after-hours = same.

Rafael Mendes: +1, but runbook needs explicit “symptoms” (p99 spike + bytes_moved correlation) to avoid flapping.

Allison Grant: I’ll add a note to the metrics standard doc for “signals that indicate compaction-caused latency” (bytes_moved burst + compaction_time_us + p99).

Omar El-Sayed: thanks. will keep eviction out unless we magically finish it early + soak. otherwise target 1.22.x follow-on.

Connor O'Brien: perfect. ping this thread when 19357 is merged so I can mark the train risk as “contained by flag + kill-switch”.

title: #eng-releases: 1764123095
preview:
Connor O'Brien: kicking off thread for Optimize quality gates beta rollout. goal: internal beta -> limited prod, with release notes + change mgmt. need owners + dates so I can book release train slots.

Talia Benson: thx. what’s target window? last I saw was “late Jan staging enforcement / Feb limited prod”. still true?

Aditya Rao: yep. internal beta: enforce in staging + a couple internal routes w/ flags week of Jan 20. limited prod for hosted API subset starting Feb 10-ish (need SRE signoff).

Sahana Iyer: from release/CI side: we’ll need a “required checks” list for anything touching gating codepaths. also need a plan for GPU scarcity for eval jobs during the beta.

Paula Mendes: rollout controller piece is in flight. release impact: canary promotion will hard-block on gate failures. we should call that out in release notes + change template.

Connor O'Brien: cool. here’s what I think we need before beta starts:
- release notes draft (internal + limited customer)
- change request template filled (owner/approver)
- runbook link + oncall prep checklist
- feature flags + rollback plan validated
- comms plan (#eng, #support, #sales)
pls confirm / add.

Sean Gallagher: add SLO + paging expectations. we don’t want oncall getting paged for flaky evals day 1. at least “alerts in ticket mode” for first week.

Allison Grant: +1. observability spec says we can start with dashboards + warning alerts, then enable paging once flake rate stable. release notes should mention “gate execution latency” can block rollouts.

Dylan Brooks: Console UI will show “Blocked by quality gate” w/ reasons + artifact links. we need copy review + support enablement because customers will ask “why can’t I apply this optimization?”

Chloe Martin: we’ll ship Console API changes behind a flag too. need to coordinate to avoid UI calling fields that aren’t there yet.

Elliot Price: I can own docs/release notes wording. question: do we want separate notes for (a) internal beta and (b) limited prod? or single doc w/ sections.

Connor O'Brien: separate. internal beta note can be more candid (“known limitations”), limited prod needs customer-safe wording + support FAQ.

ReleaseBot: Reminder: Production changes require a Change Record at least 48h in advance. Template: https://go.redwood/changes/template (include rollback + monitoring).

Talia Benson: I’ll coordinate the change record + approvals. Aditya/Paula can you give me the “go/no-go” criteria for limited prod (pass rate, flake rate, runtime bounds)?

Aditya Rao: draft criteria we discussed in #product:
- gate pass rate on internal workloads >= 90% for smoke tier; full tier pass >= 80% (expect some real fails)
- flake rate <= 5% (after reruns policy)
- p95 gate end-to-end runtime <= 25 min for full tier, <= 6 min smoke
- no P0/P1 incidents from enforcement in staging

Sean Gallagher: add: “no sustained increase in rollout pipeline timeouts” + “eval-harness queue depth within normal range”. otherwise we’ll just DDOS ourselves w/ gating.

Sahana Iyer: on GPU scarcity: we should cap concurrent gate runs per region + have a backoff. in CI, we can run a stubbed integration test suite, but the real GPU eval jobs should be nightly / pre-merge only for specific labels.

Connor O'Brien: for beta, how do we want the release mechanics?
- deploy gating enforcement service changes
- deploy eval-harness trigger endpoint
- enable flags per route
- enable rollout controller block
need ordering.

Paula Mendes: ordering suggestion:
1) ship everything dark (no enforcement)
2) enable gating runs (record-only) to validate pipeline + artifacts
3) enable enforcement at “apply” boundary in staging
4) enable rollout controller block in staging
5) internal routes in prod w/ smoke-tier only
6) expand to limited customer routes w/ smoke->full

Allison Grant: and between 2) and 3) we need dashboards live + alert rules in “no-page” mode. otherwise we’ll be blind.

Leila Farouk: QA note: we should do at least one end-to-end rehearsal with a forced fail + override + rollback. support needs screenshots + expected states.

Dylan Brooks: override UX: console shows “Override requested” -> “Override granted (expires …)”. we should include the approval group name in the UI so people know who to ping.

Talia Benson: who is approver group for beta? last email thread said tighten after near-miss.

Connor O'Brien: per latest: for limited prod, overrides require (a) Optimize PM (Aditya) + (b) Eng oncall manager or SRE delegate. internal beta can be just PM + eng lead. confirm?

Sean Gallagher: for prod, I want SRE in the loop. “SRE oncall + Optimize PM” is fine. also require expiration <= 7 days.

Aditya Rao: works. we’ll document “max 7 days, must attach evidence + follow-up task”.

Elliot Price: release notes draft outline:
- What’s new: quality gates required before Optimize config changes (batching/caching/quant)
- Impact: can block apply + can block canary promotion
- How it works: smoke tier then full tier; artifacts + deltas; reruns on flake
- Failure modes: eval timeouts, flake, true regression
- What to do: view artifacts; rerun; request override; rollback suggestion
- Known limitations (beta): limited prompt set coverage, region capacity, private deployments manual
- Contacts: #help, #eng-ml, oncall rotation
ok?

Chloe Martin: add “API behavior changes”: apply endpoint returns structured failure reason + gate_run_id. support/devs will see new error codes.

Sahana Iyer: also call out the CI requirement: any PR touching gating must pass schema validation + integration contract tests. devs will ask why merges are blocked.

Connor O'Brien: change management steps:
- Talia files change record for staging enforcement (Jan 20) + prod limited (Feb 10)
- Elliot writes internal beta release note by Jan 15; customer-safe note by Feb 1
- SRE signs off on alerts/dashboards readiness by Jan 18
- rehearsal game day Jan 22 (Leila + Dylan + SRE)
- comms: post to #eng and #support 1 week ahead
sound right?

Talia Benson: I’ll put those in a tracker doc + send calendar holds. Connor can you be DRI for the Jan 22 game day scheduling?

Connor O'Brien: yep.

Allison Grant: last thing: add a “rollback switch” runbook step. if gates start timing out, we need to disable enforcement without redeploy. ensure flag is at runtime + rollout controller level.

Paula Mendes: rollout controller already has a kill switch flag. will document.

Sean Gallagher: + confirm we won’t page on “single gate fail” (that’s expected). paging should be on “systemic failure: gate infra down, timeouts spike, pass rate cliff across many routes”.

Connor O'Brien: ok. I’ll summarize action items:
- Elliot: internal beta notes (Jan 15), limited prod notes (Feb 1)
- Talia: change records + approvals + comms plan
- Allison/Sean: dashboards/alerts ready + paging policy
- Paula: rollout controller ordering + kill switch doc
- Sahana: CI guidance + capacity caps for eval jobs
- Leila/Dylan/Chloe: Jan 22 rehearsal + support-ready artifacts/error codes
reply if anything missing.

Aditya Rao: looks good. I’ll also share go/no-go criteria in the change record so approvals have something concrete.

ReleaseBot: Change Record tip: include “customer impact assessment” and “support readiness” checkboxes. https://go.redwood/changes/checklist

Connor O'Brien: thx all. I’ll follow up w/ links once docs exist.
